title: The Japanese Robe
creator: Stevens, Alfred, 1823-1906
time period and styles: Japonisme; realism (artistic form of expression); Late Victorian
descripton: A woman in a blue kimono stands before a mirror with her back to the viewer and only partially revealing her face as she examines herself.
signficance: Exemplifies how Japanese objects and fashion were incorporated into European art, often detached from authentic context.

title: Lily
creator: Dow, Arthur W. (Arthur Wesley), 1857-1922
descripton: A vertically oriented composition of a stylized lily with bold outlines and flattened color fields
signficance: Demonstrates how Western artists applied Japanese mediums as a way to express art.

title: Japanese Man
creator: Shindler, A. Zeno (Antonio Zeno), 1823-1899
descripton: A traditionally dressed Japanese man, samurai, stands in a landscape holding a sword.
signficance: Reveals Western perceptions of Japan as both traditional and exotic during a period of increasing contact.

title: Japonisme
creator: Somm, Henry, 1844-1907
cultural elements: folding fan; paper lantern; bonnet; western dresses
descripton: A Western woman is juxtaposed with Japanese motifs, people, and architecture.
signficance: Encapsulates the European craze for Japanese art, known as Japonisme, which reshaped Western visual culture

title: Water Lily Pond
creator: Monet, Claude, 1840-1926
descripton: A pond with water lilies and a curved bridge fills the composition.
signficance: inspired by Japanese garden design and visual principles

title: The Child’s Bath
creator: Cassatt, Mary, 1844-1926
descripton: A mother washes a child in an intimate domestic scene
signficance: inspired by Japanese woodblock prints, demonstrating their impact on Impressionist technique

title: Noh Dancer
creator: La Farge, John, 1880-1963
medium and technique: drawing; wood engraving on Japan tissue
descripton: A Noh dancer in elaborate costume poses with a folding fan in a theatrical setting.
signficance: Conveys how Western audiences were intereted Japanese traditions and further fascination with Japanese aesthetics.

title: The Flowering Orchard
creator: Gogh, Vincent van, 1853-1890
descripton: A orchard scene rendered with expressive brushwork and simplified forms.
signficance: reflects his study of Japanese prints, particularly their flat color areas and emphasis on nature

title: Flowering Plum Orchard
creator: Gogh, Vincent van, 1853-1890
descripton: A direct reinterpretation of Hiroshige’s composition with intensified color and added decorative borders.
signficance: Demonstrates the deep interest in Japanese work, mimicing characters and styles
related work: The Flowering Orchard; Plum Garden at Kameido
source: Van Gogh Museum, Amsterdam (Vincent van Gogh Foundation)

title: Plum Garden at Kameido
creator: Andō, Hiroshige, 1797-1858
descripton: A close-up composition of a flowering plum tree dominates the foreground, with figures and a garden beyond.
signficance: influenced Western artists like Van Gogh and Monet, shaping modern composition

title: Peaceful Pleasures of the Highest Nobility
creator: Inoue, Yasuji, 1864-1889
cultural elements: western dresses; military uniform; straw hats (sun hats)
depicted objects: women; children; men; toys; books; furniture; garden
descripton: A multi-panel composition showing women, men, and children in a transition between exterior to interior  environment.
signficance: Illustrates Japan’s selective adoption of Western culture during modernization.

title: A Picture of Prosperity: America
creator: Utagawa, Hiroshige, 1826-1869
cultural elements: western dresses; parasols; walking canes; bonnets; Romanesque Revival
descripton: A lively urban scene depicts Westernized architecture and fashionable citizens in an American city.

title: America
creator: Hiroshige, III, 1842?-1894
descripton: Woman in western-style dress and feathered headdress rides a hourse in a snowy landscape beneath pine branches.
signficance: Foreigners were frequently depicted in dramatic or picturesque settings, reinforcing their novelty while integrating them into familiar ukiyo-e compositional formats.
related work: An American Drawn from Life; View of the Seafront in Yokohama

title: View of the Seafront in Yokohama
creator: Hiroshige, III, 1842?-1894
cultural elements: fishing vessels; naval ships; carrioles; western dresses; western flags; English language
depicted objects: harbor scene; ports; watercrafts; flags; horses
descripton: A bustling harbor scene shows foreign ships, Western-style buildings, and mixed populations in the treaty port of Yokohama.
signficance: Japan’s sustained encounters with global trade and foreigners, visualizing rapid urban and cultural transformation

title: French
creator: Utagawa, Yoshikazu, active 1848-1863
cultural elements: bonnets; western dresses; cranes; tea services
descripton: A Western couple sits indoors with tea service and furnishings,
signficance: Depicts interpretation of European domestic life, in part presenting them as "types" defined by their nationality

title: In The Mood for a Walk
creator: Taiso, Yoshitoshi, 1839-1892
cultural elements: straw hats (sun hats); western dresses; parasols; tassel; buttons; irises
descripton: Japanese woman in hybrid Western-influenced dress strolls among irises.
signficance: Demonstrates how Western clothing and leisure aesthetics were selectively absorbed into Japanese culture as symbols of modernity.

title: An American Drawn from Life
creator: Utagawa, Yoshitora, active 1850-1870
descripton: A bearded Western man rides a horse in military-style attire
signficance: Artists highlighted distinctive physical features and dress to construct recognizable, sometimes stereotyped images of foreigners for Japanese audiences

title: Dutchmen
creator: Utagawa, Yoshitora, active 1850-1870
descripton: Two European figures read and converse in an interior setting, rendered with attention to books, furniture, and dress.
signficance: Depictions of literacy, interiors, and behavior signaled Western intellectual culture and everyday life as objects of fascination
related work: French; An American Drawn from Life
